data.body:
RESCOPE + CLOSE rationale (operator-approved 2026-07-18).

The DETERMINISTIC half of E3 was designed and built: the reproducible stratified sample (runs/e3/sample.jsonl), the frozen plan-v3 baseline (runs/e3/baseline.jsonl), the 50-ticket over-sampled FP-control pool (runs/e3/fp_control_pool.jsonl), the replay driver with clone + REVIEW_RESULT-strip + manifest + drift-guard seams (replay_candidate.py), the pure LLM-free metrics library (e3_metrics.py: build_matrix, criterion_deltas, tkey, pass_to_block_flip_rate, fp_control_eval, fp_control_valid), and golden tests (test_e3_metrics.py). It lives as WIP in the cal-e3 worktree on branch calib/6982-e3 and is intentionally NOT landed on main (the driver only exists to drive the rejected batch).

The EXPENSIVE LLM BATCH REPLAY (the ~450-run live gate replay: coarse ~200 sweep + precise ~50-ticket x3 candidate + x3 current fresh runs, and the raw_verdicts.jsonl / transition_matrix.json / criterion_deltas.jsonl / fp_control.json outputs) is PERMANENTLY REJECTED on cost. It is SUPERSEDED by story d8a5 (change #798, MERGED): the standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder criterion_effectiveness.py (on main), which accumulates the same verdict-perturbation signal from real production reviews at $0 marginal LLM cost. History-MINING is also dead: only ~4 plan-v3 sidecar baselines exist vs ~200 needed (the impact_model_version bump stranded the history) - which is exactly why a live replay would have been required and why the cost was unjustifiable. No batch was run; no LLM eval spend was incurred.
